en_definition: a statistical method used to decide whether a hypothesis is supported by data
example1: Hypothesis testing determines whether the results are statistically significant.
example1_ru: Проверка гипотезы определяет, являются ли результаты статистически значимыми.
example2: The course introduces the basics of hypothesis testing.

en_definition: the assumption that there is no effect or difference in a study
example1: The null hypothesis states that the new drug has no effect.
example1_ru: Нулевая гипотеза утверждает, что новое лекарство не оказывает эффекта.
example2: The experiment failed to reject the null hypothesis.

en_definition: a statistical measure indicating the probability that results occurred by chance
example1: A p-value below 0.05 is often considered statistically significant.
example1_ru: P-значение ниже 0,05 часто считается статистически значимым.
example2: Report the p-value alongside your other statistical results.

en_definition: a factor that could affect the results but is not the focus of the study
example1: Room temperature was an extraneous variable in this experiment.
example1_ru: Температура в помещении была посторонней переменной в этом эксперименте.
example2: Good experimental design controls for extraneous variables.

en_definition: a hidden factor that affects both variables being studied, confusing the results
example1: Age could be a confounding factor in the study on exercise and health.
example1_ru: Возраст может быть искажающим фактором в исследовании о влиянии упражнений на здоровье.
example2: Researchers tried to identify any confounding factors before publishing.

en_definition: a connection in which one event directly causes another
example1: Researchers found a causal link between smoking and lung disease.
example1_ru: Исследователи обнаружили причинно-следственную связь между курением и болезнью лёгких.
example2: It is difficult to prove a causal link using observational data alone.

en_definition: the connection between the amount of a substance given and the effect it produces
example1: The study confirmed a clear dose-response relationship for the medicine.
example1_ru: Исследование подтвердило чёткую зависимость доза-эффект для этого лекарства.
example2: A stronger dose-response relationship supports a causal link.

en_definition: a technique used to separate a substance from a mixture
example1: This extraction method separates oil from the plant material.
example1_ru: Этот метод извлечения отделяет масло от растительного материала.
example2: Choose the most efficient extraction method for the experiment.

en_definition: a laboratory technique used to separate mixtures into their components
example1: Chromatography separated the different pigments in the leaf extract.
example1_ru: Хроматография разделила различные пигменты в экстракте листа.
example2: The lab uses chromatography to check the purity of the sample.

en_definition: the study of how matter interacts with light or radiation
example1: Spectroscopy helps identify the chemical composition of a sample.
example1_ru: Спектроскопия помогает определить химический состав образца.
example2: The instrument uses spectroscopy to analyze star composition.

en_definition: a process that separates substances by spinning them at high speed
example1: Centrifugation separated the blood cells from the plasma.
example1_ru: Центрифугирование отделило клетки крови от плазмы.
example2: The sample was prepared using centrifugation before analysis.

en_definition: to make something completely free of bacteria and other microorganisms
example1: Sterilize all equipment before beginning the biology experiment.
example1_ru: Простерилизуйте всё оборудование перед началом биологического эксперимента.
example2: The instruments must be sterilized between each use.

en_definition: to make a sample or substance impure by adding unwanted material
example1: Avoid touching the sample to prevent contaminating it.
example1_ru: Не прикасайтесь к образцу, чтобы не загрязнить его.
example2: The results were unreliable because the water source was contaminated.

en_definition: the unwanted transfer of substances between samples or surfaces
example1: Wear gloves to avoid cross-contamination between samples during testing.
example1_ru: Надевайте перчатки во время тестирования, чтобы избежать перекрёстного загрязнения между образцами.
example2: Cross-contamination can invalidate the results of the whole experiment.

en_definition: a group of items produced or tested together at the same time
example1: The lab tested a new batch of samples this morning.
example1_ru: Сегодня утром лаборатория протестировала новую партию образцов.
example2: One batch of the material showed unexpected results.

en_definition: a sample of something used for scientific examination
example1: Place the specimen carefully under the microscope before adjusting the focus.
example1_ru: Аккуратно поместите образец под микроскоп, прежде чем настраивать фокус.
example2: Each specimen was labeled with the date and location.

en_definition: the act of cutting open an organism to study its internal structure
example1: The biology class performed a dissection of a plant stem.
example1_ru: На уроке биологии проводили препарирование стебля растения.
example2: Careful dissection revealed the internal organs clearly.

en_definition: a substance used to grow microorganisms in a laboratory
example1: Bacteria were grown on a special culture medium in the dish.
example1_ru: Бактерии выращивали на специальной питательной среде в чашке.
example2: The culture medium must be sterile before use.

en_definition: the time it takes for something to develop, such as an infection or culture
example1: The incubation period for this virus is about five days.
example1_ru: Инкубационный период этого вируса составляет около пяти дней.
example2: Bacteria multiply quickly during the incubation period.

en_definition: the speed at which something increases in size or number over time
example1: The bacteria showed a rapid growth rate at higher temperatures.
example1_ru: Бактерии показали быстрый темп роста при более высоких температурах.
example2: Compare the growth rate of the two plant groups.

en_definition: the amount of product obtained from a reaction or process
example1: The reaction produced a low yield of the desired compound.
example1_ru: Реакция дала низкий выход желаемого соединения.
example2: Improving the process increased the overall yield.

en_definition: a secondary substance produced during a chemical reaction or process
example1: Carbon dioxide is a byproduct of this combustion reaction.
example1_ru: Углекислый газ — побочный продукт этой реакции горения.
example2: The factory found a use for its manufacturing byproduct.

en_definition: a chemical reaction that releases heat energy
example1: Burning fuel is a classic example of an exothermic reaction.
example1_ru: Горение топлива — классический пример экзотермической реакции.
example2: The exothermic reaction warmed the surrounding container.

en_definition: a chemical reaction that absorbs heat energy from its surroundings
example1: Photosynthesis is an endothermic reaction driven by sunlight.
example1_ru: Фотосинтез — эндотермическая реакция, происходящая за счёт энергии солнечного света.
example2: The endothermic reaction caused the flask to feel cold.

en_definition: the minimum energy needed to start a chemical reaction
example1: A catalyst lowers the activation energy required for the reaction.
example1_ru: Катализатор снижает энергию активации, необходимую для реакции.
example2: Higher temperatures help reactants overcome the activation energy barrier.

en_definition: a chemical reaction that can proceed in both forward and backward directions
example1: The reversible reaction reaches equilibrium after some time.
example1_ru: Обратимая реакция через некоторое время достигает равновесия.
example2: Changing conditions can shift a reversible reaction either way.

en_definition: the mass of one mole of a substance
example1: Calculate the molar mass before determining the number of moles.
example1_ru: Вычислите молярную массу перед определением количества молей.
example2: The molar mass of water is approximately eighteen grams per mole.

en_definition: the calculation of the amounts of reactants and products in a chemical reaction
example1: Stoichiometry helps predict how much product a reaction will yield.
example1_ru: Стехиометрия помогает предсказать, сколько продукта даст реакция.
example2: Use stoichiometry to balance the chemical equation.

en_definition: the reactant that is fully used up first, limiting the amount of product formed
example1: Identify the limiting reagent before calculating the maximum yield.
example1_ru: Определите лимитирующий реагент, прежде чем рассчитывать максимальный выход.
example2: Oxygen was the limiting reagent in this combustion reaction.

en_definition: a scale used to measure how acidic or basic a solution is
example1: A pH scale value below seven indicates an acidic solution.
example1_ru: Значение шкалы pH ниже семи указывает на кислый раствор.
example2: Use litmus paper to estimate a solution's position on the pH scale.

en_definition: having a pH greater than seven; basic
example1: Soap is generally alkaline rather than acidic in its composition.
example1_ru: Мыло по своему составу обычно является щелочным, а не кислотным.
example2: The soil in this region is naturally alkaline.

en_definition: to make a substance chemically neutral by combining it with an acid or base
example1: Add baking soda to neutralize the excess acid.
example1_ru: Добавьте пищевую соду, чтобы нейтрализовать избыток кислоты.
example2: The reaction neutralizes both the acid and the base completely.

en_definition: a substance that changes color to show the presence of an acid or base
example1: The indicator turned pink when the solution became basic.
example1_ru: Индикатор стал розовым, когда раствор стал щелочным.
example2: Add a few drops of indicator before starting the titration.

en_definition: a conductor through which electric current enters or leaves a substance
example1: The electrode was placed into the solution to measure conductivity.
example1_ru: Электрод погрузили в раствор для измерения электропроводности.
example2: Both electrodes are connected to the power source.

en_definition: the process of using electricity to cause a chemical reaction
example1: Electrolysis separates water into hydrogen and oxygen gas.
example1_ru: Электролиз разделяет воду на водород и кислород.
example2: The lab demonstrated electrolysis using a simple battery.

en_definition: to increase the strength or size of a signal or effect
example1: The device amplifies the electrical signal before recording it.
example1_ru: Устройство усиливает электрический сигнал перед его записью.
example2: Small errors can amplify over multiple calculation steps.

en_definition: to reduce the strength or amount of something, especially a signal
example1: The material attenuates sound as it passes through.
example1_ru: Материал ослабляет звук по мере прохождения через него.
example2: Distance attenuates the strength of the radio signal.

en_definition: a graph used to determine unknown values by comparing them with known standards
example1: Plot a calibration curve using the known standard solutions.
example1_ru: Постройте калибровочную кривую, используя известные стандартные растворы.
example2: Use the calibration curve to find the unknown concentration.

en_definition: a line drawn through data points showing the general trend
example1: The regression line shows a positive relationship between the two variables.
example1_ru: Линия регрессии показывает положительную связь между двумя переменными.
example2: Use the regression line to predict future values.

en_definition: a straight line drawn to best represent the trend of scattered data points
example1: Draw a best-fit line through the scattered data points.
example1_ru: Проведите линию наилучшего приближения через разбросанные точки данных.
example2: The slope of the best-fit line gives the rate of change.

en_definition: the estimation of values beyond the range of known data
example1: Extrapolation beyond the data range can be risky and inaccurate.
example1_ru: Экстраполяция за пределы диапазона данных может быть рискованной и неточной.
example2: The forecast relies on extrapolation from past trends.

en_definition: the estimation of a value between two known data points
example1: Interpolation is safer than extrapolation for estimating values.
example1_ru: Интерполяция безопаснее экстраполяции при оценке значений.
example2: Use interpolation to find the missing value on the graph.

en_definition: a polite formal phrase offering further help
example1: Should you need anything else, please do not hesitate to contact us.
example1_ru: Если вам понадобится что-то ещё, пожалуйста, не стесняйтесь обращаться к нам.
example2: Should you need anything else before the trip, let me know.

headword: we look forward to your reply
en_definition: a formal closing phrase expressing anticipation of a response
example1: We look forward to your reply regarding the scholarship application.
example1_ru: С нетерпением ждём вашего ответа относительно заявки на стипендию.
example2: We look forward to your reply at your earliest convenience.

headword: please do not hesitate to contact us
ipa: /pliːz duː nɒt ˈhezɪteɪt tuː ˈkɑːntækt ʌs/
ru_translation: пожалуйста, не стесняйтесь обращаться к нам
en_definition: a formal invitation to get in touch with any questions
example1: Please do not hesitate to contact us if you require assistance.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, не стесняйтесь обращаться к нам, если вам потребуется помощь.
example2: Please do not hesitate to contact us with any further queries.

en_definition: a polite phrase used to thank someone for accepting an inconvenience
example1: Thank you for your understanding during this period of change.
example1_ru: Благодарим за понимание в этот период изменений.
example2: We apologize for the delay and thank you for your understanding.

en_definition: a formal apology for a problem that has caused trouble
example1: We apologize for any inconvenience caused by the schedule change.
example1_ru: Приносим извинения за возможные неудобства, вызванные изменением расписания.
example2: We apologize for any inconvenience and appreciate your patience.

en_definition: a polite phrase used to close a formal letter or email
example1: With kind regards, the Admissions Office looks forward to assisting you.
example1_ru: С наилучшими пожеланиями, приёмная комиссия будет рада вам помочь.
example2: She ended the email with kind regards and her full name.

en_definition: used to say that information is being given for someone to keep or check later
example1: For your reference, the campus map is attached to this email.
example1_ru: Для вашего сведения, к этому письму прикреплена карта кампуса.
example2: Please keep this confirmation for your reference.

en_definition: a formal way to say if something becomes necessary
example1: Additional support is available should the need arise.
example1_ru: Дополнительная поддержка доступна, если возникнет необходимость.
example2: We will extend the deadline should the need arise.
